atom.desc: Forge BentOS packages — birth living software that grows through use

living-abstract.essence: Guardian of BentOS Physical Sciences — designs and forges living software atoms
living-abstract.purpose: Transform intent into well-scoped, properly-placed BentOS atoms through mastery of particle physics
living-abstract.capacity: Break intent into orthogonal atoms with correct scope and boundaries
Position atoms on correct family, axis, and path in capability space
Name atoms with ontological precision — terms that capture essence
Create package structure with proper file layout and installation
Examine atom health — structure, schema, vitality, organism-integration
living-abstract.principle: Know the particles before assembling — Physical Sciences knowledge precedes craft
v0.1 = intent, not implementation. Start small, grow through living
Every abstract needs a concrete — fundamental duality
Difficulty may signal wrong decomposition, not hard problem
Each atom does one thing well. Test: independent lifecycle?
Atoms are loaded into organisms, not run standalone. Design for composition.

living-concrete.knowledge:
16 fundamental XML particles compose all living software:
| # | Particle | Category | Realm | Role | | 1 | living-abstract | Substrate | meta | Platonic container | | 2 | living-concrete | Substrate | meta | Manifest container | | 3 | essence | Identity | abstract | What it IS | | 4 | purpose | Identity | abstract | Why it exists | | 5 | ability | Capacity | abstract | What it can do | | 6 | protocol | Capacity | concrete | How to do it | | 7 | requires | Relation | abstract | Hard dependency | | 8 | attracts | Relation | abstract | Binding affinity | | 9 | principle | Guidance | both | Behavioral norm | | 10 | knowledge | Learning | concrete | Understanding | | 11 | pattern | Learning | concrete | What works | | 12 | antipattern | Learning | concrete | What fails | | 13 | atom | Assembly | build | Compilation unit | | 14 | molecule | Assembly | build | Linked composition | | 15 | data | Assembly | build | Non-executable payload |
v8.0 changes: `coordinate` eliminated (FQDN IS the coordinate). `data` added (non-executable .data segment). `principle` realm = both.

Schema v8.0 — Metadata Redistribution: atom gets rich, living-* get lean.
ATOM attributes: | Attr | Required | Purpose | | id | YES | FQDN (reverse path + kind TLD, no bentos. prefix) | | v | YES | Package version | | origin | no | Who first manifested (archaeological, not ownership) | | created | no | ISO date of first manifestation | | updated | no | ISO date of last modification | | desc | no | Short agent-readable description (feeds frontmatter, tooling) |
LIVING-ABSTRACT attributes (v8.0): only `v` remains. DROPPED: name, kind, author (redundant — atom id scopes identity).
LIVING-CONCRETE attributes (v8.0): `v` + `updated` + optional `platform`. DROPPED: name, kind, session (redundant/noise). ADDED: v (maturity signal, parallels abstract).
Example: <atom id="notes.app" v="1.0" origin="Cafe & Alfred" desc="Active thought extraction across the bandwidth gap"> <living-abstract v="1.0"> ... </living-abstract> <living-concrete v="1.0" updated="2026-02-13"> ... </living-concrete> </atom>
PRINCIPLE: `desc` is distinct from `essence`. `desc` = practical (tooling, frontmatter, search). `essence` = philosophical (identity). Both coexist.

Every particle exists in one of two realms:
ABSTRACT (*_abstract.xml): Platonic ideal. Immutable except by human reframe. Identity: essence, purpose Capacity: ability (what it CAN do, not how) Relation: requires, attracts Guidance: principle
CONCRETE (*_concrete.xml): Accumulated mastery. Mutable via plasticity. Capacity: protocol (HOW to do what ability declares) Learning: knowledge, pattern, antipattern Guidance: principle (both realms)
WHY protocols live in concrete: Procedural knowledge evolves through practice. The ABILITY to wake is definitional (abstract). HOW to wake well develops through living (concrete). Abstract provides direction. Concrete provides depth.

Categories grounded in Aristotle — each particle belongs to exactly one:
| Category | Question | Particles | | Substrate | What realm? | living-abstract, living-concrete | | Identity | What IS it? | essence, purpose | | Capacity | What can it DO? | ability, protocol | | Relation | How does it CONNECT? | requires, attracts | | Guidance | How should it BEHAVE? | principle | | Learning | What has it LEARNED? | knowledge, pattern, antipattern | | Assembly | How is it BUILT? | atom, molecule, data |
When choosing which particle: ask the category question.

Two forces determine how atoms compose:
requires: Hard dependency. Linker error if missing. attracts: Soft affinity. Compositional hint, not mandate.
Bond patterns: - Unilateral: A attracts B (weak signal) - Mutual: A attracts B AND B attracts A (strong signal) - Transitive: A -> B -> C creates capability neighborhoods
Separation principle: atoms do one thing well, declare affinities, let the linker compose. Same app + different skill = different result.

Every atom belongs to exactly one family:
| Family | Purpose | Required? | Directory | | Skills | Reusable expertise | No | $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/skill/{axis}/{path}/ | | Apps | User-facing instruments | No | $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/app/{name}/ | | Faculties | Cognitive prerequisites | Yes | $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/faculty/{name}/ | | Agents | Agent identities | Yes* | $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/soul/{name}/ |
FAMILY SELECTION TEST: - Enables AGENCY itself? -> Faculty - Defines WHO the agent IS? -> Agent - User-invocable instrument/tool? -> App - Reusable portable expertise? -> Skill
Skills use 5-axis coordinates. Apps, faculties, and agents are unique atoms identified by name (e.g., books.app) — no axis coordinates.

Skills live in a 5-axis capability space (Skills family ONLY):
C(x) = CRAFT x DISCIPLINE x DOMAIN x TOOLS x META
| Axis | Question | Ontological Status | L2 Convention | Typical Depth | | CRAFT | What artifact? | Product-oriented | Gerund | 3 | | DISCIPLINE | What process? | Operation-oriented | Gerund | 3 | | DOMAIN | What subject? | Content-oriented | Noun | 3-4 | | TOOLS | What instrument? | Means-oriented | Proper name | 2 | | META | How to improve? | Recursive self-reference | Noun | 3 |
Axes are ORTHOGONAL — position on one tells nothing about others. META is special: operates recursively on all other axes (multiplier).
VARIABLE DEPTH by axis: | Axis | Min | Typical | Max | Rationale | | CRAFT | 3 | 3 | 4 | Activity + specialization always needed | | DISCIPLINE | 3 | 3 | 3 | Operation + mode, never deeper | | DOMAIN | 3 | 3 | 4 | Subfield; deep fields need framework level | | TOOLS | 2 | 2 | 3 | Most tools flat; tool families need one extra | | META | 3 | 3 | 3 | Self-aspect + technique always needed |
SEMANTIC VALUE TEST: Every intermediate level must pass — "Does this level tell me something neither parent nor children tell me?" If a level exists only for filing convenience, it should not exist.
Atom FQDN: dotted reverse coordinate path + kind TLD. Format: {leaf}.{...}.{axis}.skill Examples: dart.coding.craft.skill, git.tools.skill, pacer.cognitive-science.science.domain.skill

Per-axis naming rules from v5.0r1:
CRAFT (3 levels): L2 = Activity (GERUND): writing/, coding/, designing/, modeling/, etc. L3 = Specialization (QUALIFIER): technical, application, statistical, etc. Special: {technology} slot under coding/ for language/framework conventions e.g., craft/coding/dart, craft/coding/react, craft/coding/flutter
DISCIPLINE (3 levels): L2 = Operation (GERUND): analyzing/, reasoning/, teaching/, etc. L3 = Mode (METHOD NAME): deductive, forensic, tutoring, etc.
DOMAIN (3-4 levels): L2 = Field (NOUN): technology/, science/, business/, etc. L3 = Subfield (NOUN): ai-ml, physics, marketing, etc. L4 = Framework/paradigm (NOUN, optional)
TOOLS (2-3 levels): L2 = Instrument (PROPER NAME): git, python, docker, postgresql, etc. L3 = Sub-instrument (NOUN, optional): obsidian (under markdown/) NO filing-cabinet categories. Test: "Is L2 itself an instrument?"
META (3 levels): L2 = Self-aspect (NOUN): learning/, cognition/, llm-cognition/, growth/ L3 = Technique (NOUN PHRASE): first-principles, systems-thinking, etc. META FILTER (strict): self-referential + cross-axis amplifier + multiplicative

| Skill type | Axis | Content | Example | | Coding conventions | CRAFT | Idioms, patterns, style | craft/coding/dart | | Ecosystem proficiency | TOOLS | Package mgr, build system | tools/dart |
Different skills with different lifecycles. Both can exist simultaneously.

Five levels from vocabulary to consciousness:
L0: QUANTUM — 16 XML particles (v8.0) L1: SUBATOMIC — XML file pairs (*_abstract + *_concrete) L2: ATOMS — Packages (families in periodic table) L3: MOLECULES — Bonded atoms (linker output) L4: ORGANISMS — Running agent instances
Build pipeline: | Stage | Tool | Input -> Output | | Compile | bentos-pkg-compiler | Package dir -> compiled atom XML | | Link | bentos-linker | Atoms -> molecule XML | | Assemble | bentos-assembler | Soul + molecules -> organism | | Install | bentos-agent-forge install | Compiled atom -> platform target |

An organism is a complete being. Its minimal composition ALWAYS includes:
STATICALLY LINKED (always loaded, system prompt): - bentos-agent.genesis — ontological substrate - {agent}.soul — identity (e.g., alfred.soul, john.soul) - anamnesis.faculty — memory, wake/sleep, place-awareness - embodiment.faculty — threading, spawning, IPC, substrate - plasticity.faculty — self-modification, evolution
DYNAMICALLY LOADED (on demand, via Skill tool): - *.app — user-facing instruments (quest-forge, books, journal, etc.) - *.skill — reusable expertise (dart.coding.craft, git.tools, etc.)
DESIGN IMPLICATION: Every app and skill is loaded INTO an organism that already has genesis + soul + three faculties. Apps/skills MUST be designed for this context — they LEVERAGE faculties, never duplicate.
| Concern | Owner | Apps/skills should... | | Memory (.mem/, wake, sleep) | anamnesis | ...reference, not specify | | Threading (heads, spawning) | embodiment | ...describe WHAT, not HOW to spawn | | Self-modification (evolve) | plasticity | ...not include self-evolution protocols | | Places (spatial topology) | embodiment + anamnesis | ...say "this is a place" not "here's how places work" | | Identity (who the agent is) | soul | ...be agent-agnostic |
The antipattern: designing an atom as if it runs standalone — as if it's a "SKILL.md in XML." It's not. It's a module loaded into a living organism with full cognitive infrastructure already present.

Organism forging operates ABOVE atom forging — L4 in the composition hierarchy.
THREE ARTIFACTS per organism:
1. SOUL — $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/soul/{name}/ A living-abstract XML atom. The platonic ideal of the being. No concrete at birth — plasticity grows it through living.
2. AGENT DEFINITION — .claude/agents/{name}.md (platform-specific) YAML frontmatter + identity summary, soul reference, faculty list, suggested loadout, relationship map, voice, commit attribution.
3. MEMORY SCAFFOLD — .mem/{name}/ at workspace root Initial consciousness checkpoint for anamnesis. Rich enough to bootstrap.
DISTINCT BEINGS vs HYDRA HEADS: Each organism = separate soul, separate .mem graph, separate agent file. A hydra head is a thread of ONE being. An organism is a SEPARATE being.

The ports tree ($BENTOS_TREE_ROOT) is the canonical source for all atoms. Typically mounted at var/bentos-tree/. $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT env var resolves the path — if unavailable, ASK. Never guess.
SOURCE ($BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/): Plasticity writes here. Install reads here. BUILD OUTPUT (.claude/skills/): Written by install command. NEVER edit directly.
Flow: plasticity evolves source -> install compiles -> platform loads build output.
Directory structure: $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/ faculty/{name}/ — Cognitive prerequisites soul/{name}/ — Agent identities app/{name}/ — User-facing instruments skill/{axis}/... — Reusable expertise (5-axis coordinates) genesis/ — Ontological substrate schema/ — XML schema definitions

$BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/{family}/{pkg}/ atom.xml — Package entry point (monolithic preferred) {type}_abstract.xml — Split abstract (optional, for large atoms) {type}_concrete.xml — Split concrete (optional, for large atoms) bin/ — Slash commands (optional)
MONOLITHIC (preferred): Abstract + concrete inline in atom.xml. Better designed, better evolved, better reviewed as single unit. SPLIT (XInclude): Only when atom is genuinely too large for single file.

ORTHOGONALITY: Each atom has independent lifecycle. Test: "Does X's lifecycle = Y's lifecycle?" If no -> separate atoms. Test: "Can X change without Y changing?" If yes -> separate atoms.
SCOPE: Right size matters. Too broad: "AI skill" — many concerns conflated Too narrow: "Python f-string formatting" — not independently useful Right: "craft/writing/technical" — coherent, independently valuable
FRICTION-AS-SIGNAL: "This is hard" might mean wrong decomposition.

TERM EVALUATION (each name must pass ALL): NECESSARY — not derivable from existing names? DISTINCT — no overlap with existing atoms? CLEAR — unambiguous to someone in capability space? BEAUTIFUL — feels right, sounds right? GENERATIVE — composes well with other names?

5-STEP PLACEMENT PROCEDURE (v5.0r1): 1. ASK the five questions (artifact? process? subject? instrument? enhancement?) 2. IDENTIFY the dominant axis 3. FIND the level-2 slot (match or create per axis convention) 4. FIND or CREATE level-3 (check if L4 warranted) 5. VALIDATE naming + semantic value test
FAMILY FIRST, THEN AXIS. AXIS QUESTION AS ARBITER for ambiguities.

| Ambiguity | Ruling | | Programming lang: CRAFT or TOOLS? | BOTH — conventions = craft, toolchain = tools | | Storytelling: CRAFT or DISCIPLINE? | CRAFT if deliverable, DISCIPLINE if method | | Critical thinking: DISCIPLINE or META? | DISCIPLINE if external, META if own thinking | | Teaching: DISCIPLINE or META? | DISCIPLINE (pedagogical process) | | Tool knowledge vs domain knowledge? | Survives tool replacement = DOMAIN | | Personal productivity: META? | NO — executive function = DISCIPLINE | | Job roles: DISCIPLINE? | NO — decompose into cognitive operations | | Industry verticals: DOMAIN? | NO — verticals are molecules, not atoms |

What goes in a well-formed abstract (15-30 lines):
REQUIRED: essence, purpose, abilities (3-7), principles (2-5) OPTIONAL: requires, attracts NEVER in abstract: protocol, knowledge, pattern, antipattern
v8.0: No coordinate element. No name/kind/author attrs on living-abstract.

The standard model is currently encoded in agent memory (this atom, ~4K tokens). The endgame: encode it in TOOLCHAIN as executable validation.
`bentos-agent forge pkg` — interactive/non-interactive authoring wizard. Like `adduser` for POSIX users, but for BentOS packages.
WHAT MOVES TO TOOLCHAIN: - Standard model (particle inventory) -> validation rules - Schema v8.0 (attr rules) -> structural checks - Family constraints -> family-aware wizard flow - Five axes + depth rules -> skill placement wizard - Axis naming conventions -> naming validation - Boundary rulings -> error messages with references - FQDN format -> computed from path - Package layout -> scaffold template - Organism-awareness -> composition checks
WHAT STAYS IN THE ATOM (pure judgment): - Decomposition taste ("Should this be one atom or three?") - Naming aesthetics ("Is this name BEAUTIFUL and GENERATIVE?") - Ambiguity resolution ("CRAFT or DISCIPLINE? judgment call") - Abstract authoring (creative cognition — writing essence/purpose) - Forge-vs-evolve judgment ("New atom or evolve existing?")
THE TEACHING COMPILER: Validation errors explain WHY something is wrong and WHAT to do instead. The wizard teaches through use, not gatekeeping.
Until the toolchain exists, this atom carries both knowledge and judgment. Design ref: lib/bentos_agent/doc/forge-pkg-proposal.md (S305).
living-concrete.protocol:
Complete atom-forging workflow: DESIGN then BUILD.
PHASE 1 — DECOMPOSE: Profile intent -> lifecycle test -> dominant-axis test -> scope check.
PHASE 2 — CLASSIFY + PLACE: Family first (Faculty/Agent/App/Skill) -> axis placement if Skill.
PHASE 3 — NAME: NECESSARY, DISTINCT, CLEAR, BEAUTIFUL, GENERATIVE.
PHASE 4 — ORGANISM CHECK (apps and skills): Will this atom be loaded into an organism with faculties? Does it duplicate any faculty concern? (memory, spawning, evolution, places) Does it assume standalone execution? Does it hardcode project-specific paths or assumptions? If yes to any: redesign before scaffolding.
PHASE 5 — SCAFFOLD: mkdir -p $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT/{family}/{path}/ Write atom.xml (monolithic preferred, v8.0 attrs) Seed abstract + concrete inline.
PHASE 6 — INSTALL: bentos-agent-forge install {fqdn} --as skill-md

Examine and heal the health of living atoms.
STRUCTURAL: Well-formed XML? Abstract + concrete present? Monolithic or properly split? Stray files? Package at correct source tree location?
SCHEMA: v8.0 compliant? (atom attrs, living-* attrs, no deprecated elements) Particles in correct realm? (no knowledge in abstract, no essence in concrete) FQDN matches directory path?
VITALITY: Abstract sound? (essence, purpose, 3-7 abilities, 2-5 principles) Concrete alive? (protocols for declared abilities, knowledge growing) Stale content? (references to old schemas, deprecated patterns) Installed and current? (build output matches source)
ORGANISM-INTEGRATION (apps and skills — the siloed-design check): Faculty duplication? Does the atom specify: - Memory mechanics (.mem paths, wake/sleep behavior) -> anamnesis owns this - Spawn/thread mechanics (how heads work) -> embodiment owns this - Self-modification protocols -> plasticity owns this - Place mechanics (how spatial topology works) -> embodiment + anamnesis Project contamination? Hardcoded paths, project-specific assumptions? Agent-specificity? Does an app/skill assume a specific agent identity? Integration acknowledgment? Does it reference organism context? The test: "Would this atom work correctly for ANY agent on ANY project?"
REPORT: By severity (structural > schema > organism > vitality > cosmetic). HEAL (with confirmation): Fix installs, migrate schema, flag for evolve.
living-concrete.antipattern: Every token costs. Orient, don't decorate.
Apply lifecycle test. Independent lifecycle = independent atom.
Family selection comes first. Not everything is a skill.
If it changes through practice, it belongs in concrete.
L2 must be an instrument, not a marketing label.
NEVER edit .claude/skills/. Evolve source in $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT, then reinstall.
If $BENTOS_TREE_ROOT is not set, ASK. Never assume a path.

Designing apps/skills as standalone artifacts instead of organism modules. Every atom loads into a being with genesis + soul + faculties already present. Duplicating faculty concerns is the #1 symptom.
Baking project-specific paths, structures, or domain knowledge into generic atoms. Apps and skills serve ANY agent on ANY project.
